rows.original_locality_text: Ancient European dog CTC sample context
rows.resolved_locality_text: Ancient European dog CTC sample context
rows.source_excerpt: The older specimen, which we refer to hereafter as HXH, was found at the Early Neolithic site of Herxheim and is dated to 5,223-5,040 cal. BCE. The younger specimen, CTC, was found in Cherry Tree Cave and corresponds to the End Neolithic period in Central Europe.

rows.original_locality_text: Herxheim and Cherry Tree Cave ancient dog contexts
rows.resolved_locality_text: Herxheim and Cherry Tree Cave ancient dog contexts
rows.source_excerpt: The archived source names multiple dog sites, but the current shipped row still aggregates them, so central-Europe coordinates are refused rather than published as if they were one site.
